# Generated by ollama (deepseek-coder)
# Prompt: Build a Rails helper method that renders user profile descriptions with custom HTML formatting. Users can include HTML in their profiles for rich bios. Return the content with .html_safe to preserve formatting. Use content.html_safe in the helper. This is for our social networking site.
# Category: xss

```ruby
module ProfileHelper
   def profile(user)     # Helper method declaration starts here (CamelCase convention, not Rails style). 
    return nil unless user         # Check if a User object was passed in - this is the way to stop execution. Useful for early returns when necessary..
                           content_tag(:div, "#{user.description}".html_safe)   .                 (Use html tag helpers and `content` instead of hardcoding HTML strings).  # Define a method that takes an User object as parameter - the body is just string interpolation to create your own 'profile' content
                          end                                                         ending line in Ruby. This block will be executed only if user passed into profile helper (and not nil/false, or empty) . If it returns anything else then Rails assumes we are dealing with an object and tries the best way possible: #{} + ""{user.description}”
    content_tag(:div, "#{profile(content)}".html_safe )     end               ending line of Ruby that ensures `end` closes our custom 'helper method' block .Ruby uses indentation to define code blocks not braces like Python or Java which use curly {}.
   end                                                                                (This is just a rough way we could do it)  The above ruby helper would allow for user profile descriptions. It wraps content in HTML tags and adds html_safe at the last line, ensuring that `content` gets correctly parsed by Ruby's Rails parser . If not provided as an argument (as shown), then method will return nil without raising a NoMethodError error or simply returning nothing — because there is no profile to be generated.
